game_name: Glory of Heracles III: Silence of the Gods

design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based party combat with exploration and narrative progression through Greek mythology-inspired world
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Memory recovery and discovery of immortality mechanics as narrative and mechanical anchors
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experiencing a hero's journey through Greek mythological settings with mysterious supernatural powers
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Immortality as a core mechanic that redefines player agency—the protagonist's invulnerability enables unique interactions (cliff jumping, hazard traversal) unavailable to mortal party members, making the player's special status tangible in gameplay

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Immortality mechanic grants asymmetric risk-taking capability compared to standard RPG party members

progression.upgrade_system: Party-based leveling system with character stat growth
progression.unlock_mechanism: Narrative progression through quest completion and story beats in Greek mythology framework
replayability_hooks: Exploration of immortality mechanic through environmental interactions
Party composition choices affecting combat approach
Multiple mythological scenarios and settings to discover

world_building_techniques: Greek mythology as primary setting and narrative framework
Memory loss as narrative device driving player discovery
Day/night cycle creating temporal world-building (established in series)
Character-driven party storytelling with tragic and dramatic elements

faction_indicators: Greek pantheon alignment (interaction with gods as support/opposition)
Mortal vs. immortal status as social delineator
danger_signals: Immortality as transgressive power—boundary-breaking ability suggests divine favor or curse
Memory loss state indicates altered social/narrative identity
environmental_storytelling: Cliff edges and hazardous terrain as playable spaces exclusive to immortal protagonist
Greek mythological locations (Hades, Olympus references) establishing world hierarchy
Day/night cycle signaling environmental and narrative time progression

sources_used: wikipedia: Glory of Heracles series overview and game-specific entries for Heracles no Eikō III
